title: Algorithms
description: What algorithms are, why they matter, and how to write step-by-step instructions to solve problems.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now

Answer these five starter questions about algorithms.
steps.js: createMCQSet('algo-dna',[{q:'What is an algorithm?',opts:['A set of step-by-step instructions to solve a problem','A type of computer hardware','A drawing tool','A random list of words'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - an algorithm is a set of steps that solves a problem.',wrongFeedback:'An algorithm is an ordered set of instructions used to solve a problem.'},{q:'Which is the best example of an algorithm?',opts:['A recipe with numbered steps','A photo of a cake','A single random word','A folder name'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - a recipe is a clear sequence of steps.',wrongFeedback:'A good real-life algorithm has steps, like a recipe or directions.'},{q:'Why does the order of steps matter?',opts:['The wrong order can produce the wrong result','Computers read backwards','It only matters for pictures','Order never matters'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - changing the order can change the outcome.',wrongFeedback:'Algorithms are ordered. Steps in the wrong order can break the solution.'},{q:'Which instruction is most precise?',opts:['Move forward 5 steps','Move a bit','Do the thing','Go over there'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - it says exactly what to do.',wrongFeedback:'Precise instructions avoid vague words like a bit or over there.'},{q:'What does finite mean for an algorithm?',opts:['It has a clear end','It goes on forever','It must use colour','It has no order'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - finite means it eventually stops.',wrongFeedback:'A finite algorithm has a clear end point.'}]);

steps.title: What is an Algorithm?
steps.content:
What is an algorithm?

An algorithm is a precise, ordered sequence of instructions that solves a problem.

Algorithm = step-by-step instructions to solve a problem

A good algorithm must be:

PropertyMeaningExample
PreciseNo vague steps✓ "Boil for 10 minutes"
✗ "Cook for a while"
OrderedSteps in the right sequenceSocks before shoes, not after
FiniteHas a clear end"Arrive at school" — stops there

steps.title: Sequencing Challenge
steps.content:
Put it in order

Click the steps in the correct order from first to last. Wrong click resets the challenge.
steps.js:
(function(){var chs=[{q:'Making a bowl of cereal:',steps:['Pour milk into the bowl','Get a bowl from the cupboard','Pick up a spoon and eat','Pour cereal into the bowl'],ans:[1,3,0,2]},{q:'Crossing the road safely:',steps:['Walk straight across','Look right','Stop at the kerb','Look left','Check both ways are clear'],ans:[2,1,3,4,0]},{q:'Logging into a computer:',steps:['Type your password','Click Log in','Turn on the monitor','Type your username','See the desktop'],ans:[2,3,0,1,4]}];var cur=0,seq=[];function shuffle(a){var b=a.slice();for(var i=b.length-1;i>0;i--){var j=Math.floor(Math.random()*(i+1));var t=b[i];b[i]=b[j];b[j]=t;}return b;}function render(){var ch=chs[cur];var c=document.getElementById('algo-seq');var avail=shuffle(ch.steps.map(function(s,i){return{s:s,i:i};}));c.innerHTML='

Challenge '+(cur+1)+' of '+chs.length+'

'+ch.q+'

';var bd=document.getElementById('algo-btns');avail.forEach(function(item){var btn=document.createElement('button');btn.className='ex-btn';btn.style.cssText='text-align:left;width:100%';btn.textContent=item.s;btn.dataset.idx=item.i;btn.onclick=function(){if(btn.disabled)return;var fb=document.getElementById('algo-fb');if(parseInt(btn.dataset.idx)===ch.ans[seq.length]){btn.style.opacity='0.35';btn.disabled=true;seq.push(parseInt(btn.dataset.idx));if(seq.length===ch.steps.length){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct order!';seq=[];if(cur

steps.title: Quick Check
steps.content: Quick check
steps.js: createMCQSet('algo-exam',[{q:'What is an algorithm?',opts:['A type of computer hardware','A set of step-by-step instructions to solve a problem','A programming language','A random list of numbers'],correct:1,feedback:'Correct.',wrongFeedback:'An algorithm is a precise, ordered set of instructions that solves a problem.'},{q:'"Cook it until it looks right" — which property does this break?',opts:['Finite','Precise','Ordered','None — it is fine'],correct:1,feedback:'Correct — it is vague, not precise.',wrongFeedback:'"Looks right" is vague. A good algorithm must be precise.'},{q:'Why must algorithm steps be in the correct order?',opts:['It looks neater','The wrong order produces the wrong result','Computers read bottom to top','Order does not matter'],correct:1,feedback:'Correct.',wrongFeedback:'The wrong order produces the wrong result — try putting your socks on after your shoes!'}]);

options: A set of step-by-step instructions to solve a problem

quizQuestions.q: Which property means an algorithm has a clear start and end?
Which is the best-written algorithm step?
options: Boil 500 ml of water for exactly 3 minutes

quizQuestions.q: A recipe is an example of an algorithm because...
options: It gives ordered steps to produce a meal

quizQuestions.q: What word describes an algorithm that has a clear start and end?
Algorithms are used in everyday life. Which of these is NOT an algorithm?
